behavior_summary: This script implements a guard NPC with a short 'U8P_GUARD1::look' description, a trigger-area reaction, and a multi-branch talk (U8P_GUARD1::use) interaction.
U8P_GUARD1::look: when the player inspects the guard it checks Npc::isDead(this) and barks either a dead-guard or living-guard short description.

U8P_GUARD1::enterFastArea / U8P_GUARD1::leaveFastArea: manage fast-area entry/exit. On entry (if guard is alive) the script clears a transient engine reference and invokes a movement/response utility (U8P_GUARD1::func1033). On exit it clears the same reference as cleanup.

U8P_GUARD1::use: a dialogue-driven conversation process presenting a dynamic menu. It tracks first-meeting and annoyance state (guard1met, guard1pissed), adapts menu options (adds/removes player-name option, town/gate topics), can put the avatar briefly in stasis, triggers animations/pathfinding, and sets/clears flags to persist conversation state.

U8P_GUARD1::func1033: utility that attempts a short pathfind/movement, waits for the path process, checks process_result(), and spawns small helper processes and calls engine helpers depending on success or failure.
notable_control_flow: U8P_GUARD1::look branches on Npc::isDead(this) to choose a short inspection/bark line.
U8P_GUARD1::enterFastArea checks Npc::isDead(this) and skips movement/response if dead; otherwise it resets an engine ref and calls U8P_GUARD1::func1033.
U8P_GUARD1::use contains a conversation loop driven by a menu array (local2) and a suspend-assigned choice variable (local1). The loop ends when the player selects the 'Bye ' option.
Dialogue branches vary by global flags guard1met and guard1pissed (first-meet vs repeat, annoyed vs calm), and by toranDead (inclusion of 'Open the gate!' option).
U8P_GUARD1::func1033 issues an Npc::pathfind, suspends to wait, then tests process_result() to follow success/failure paths and spawn different helper processes.
open_questions: What exact effects do the spawn-codes passed to FREE::func1C19 have (sound, animation, or cleanup)? — appears spawn helper but exact purpose is unknown.
Precise gameplay meaning of xavierSitting: code reads and also clears it, suggesting a special-case starting state (inferred but not fully certain).
What concrete behavior results from the guard pathfind in U8P_GUARD1::func1033 (distance/orientation offsets used) and how it looks in-game (inferred movement but not fully specified).
Exact contents and phrasing of all dynamic menu entries not exhaustively enumerated here (menu construction inferred from strings but low-level layout not fully shown).

main_function.name: U8P_GUARD1::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description when the player examines/looks at the guard; choose distinct text for alive vs dead.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the game's 'U8P_GUARD1::look' / examine action on the guard NPC.
Branches immediately on Npc::isDead(this) to select the proper short description.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD1::enterFastArea
function_map.role: fast-area entry handler: cleanup & trigger movement/response by calling U8P_GUARD1::func1033 when guard alive

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD1::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: fast-area exit handler: perform cleanup/reset of transient engine refs

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD1::use
function_map.role: player interaction/dialogue process: conversation loop, menu handling, flag updates and animation/path triggers

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD1::func1033
function_map.role: utility for short pathfind/movement and response; waits for process_result() and spawns helpers

internal_calls.purpose: Invoke guard movement/response utility when an actor enters the fast-area trigger and the guard is alive.

external_calls.purpose: Test whether the NPC is dead to select the appropriate U8P_GUARD1::look/bark string.
Clear/reset a transient engine/process reference (ref id 0x0205) before invoking follow-up behavior.
Cleanup/reset transient engine/process reference on fast-area exit.
Initiate a conversation with the player (no-map variant) at the start of the dialog flow.
Alternate engine call to begin conversation used in some branches.
Engine/NPC helper invoked during the interaction sequence (likely timing/state helper called after movement/animation).
Trigger NPC animation or orientation change during certain dialogue branches.
Temporarily freeze the player's controls/avatar during a specific dialogue branch (xavierSitting path).
Request a short movement/path action for the guard; U8P_GUARD1::func1033 waits for the spawned path process and checks process_result().
Spawn a small helper process (different spawn-codes used for success/failure branches); likely handles short-lived effects/cleanup/animation.
Called after a successful pathfind/spawn; likely coordinates follow-up animation or state.

reads.name: guard1met
reads.likely_effect: Determine whether the player is a first-time interlocutor to present first-meeting greeting and menu options.

reads.name: guard1pissed
reads.likely_effect: Select an annoyed greeting/branch and alter available responses; affects tone.

reads.name: toranDead
reads.likely_effect: Gate whether the 'Open the gate!' menu option is presented (if Toran isn't dead the gate option is offered).

reads.name: xavierSitting
reads.likely_effect: Select an alternate start sequence/greeting (a special-case state that is also cleared in one branch).

reads.name: guard1town
reads.likely_effect: Track whether the guard already described the town to avoid repeating the full town description.

writes.name: guard1met
writes.likely_effect: Set when the player first meets the guard so future interactions U8P_GUARD1::use repeat-greeting branches.

writes.name: guard1pissed
writes.likely_effect: Set/cleared depending on conversation branches to mark annoyance and influence subsequent tone/lines.

writes.name: xavierSitting
writes.likely_effect: Cleared (set to 0) in a branch that handles the xavierSitting start sequence, removing that special-case state.

writes.name: guard1town
writes.likely_effect: Set after the guard explains the town to prevent repeating the same exposition.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements a guard NPC with a short 'U8P_GUARD1::look' description, a trigger-area reaction, and a multi-branch talk (U8P_GUARD1::use) interaction.
